channel.title: Kapil Uthra — Blog
channel.description: Technical writing on OpenText ECM, AI/RAG pipelines, cloud migrations, and enterprise architecture.

item.title: Agentic ECM: When Your AI Decides Which Documents to Fetch
item.description: Single-shot RAG can't answer multi-step enterprise queries. Agentic ECM lets an LLM reason over your content repository in multiple steps — fetching, inspecting, and following document relationships until it has an answer. Tool design, guardrails, and three real patterns.

item.title: GraphRAG on OpenText: Building a Knowledge Graph from Your Content Repository
item.description: Flat vector search hits a ceiling when queries require multi-hop reasoning. GraphRAG uses the relationships already embedded in your ECM structure to answer questions that semantic search can't touch.

item.title: ACL-Aware RAG: Why Your Enterprise AI Must Respect Document Permissions
item.description: Naive RAG ignores ACLs and leaks confidential documents across user queries. The architecture for pre-retrieval filtering with a two-tier permission cache, and why post-retrieval filtering is dangerous.

item.title: OTDS SSO Deep Dive: The Authentication Layer Most xECM Implementations Get Wrong
item.description: OTCSTicket vs OTDS federation, user partition pitfalls, the login-attribute mapping trap, and why co-locating OTDS with Content Server causes intermittent auth failures under load.

item.title: From RAG to Agents: Building a Tool-Calling LLM over OpenText Content Server
item.description: How to wrap OTCS operations as LangChain tools, build a ReAct agent that decides what to search, and handle the ACL enforcement and prompt injection risks that make enterprise agents different from demos.

item.title: RAG Evaluation with RAGAS: Measuring What Actually Matters on an Enterprise OTCS Corpus
item.description: How to generate ground truth, run faithfulness/relevancy/recall metrics, and use scores to make concrete pipeline improvements — with baseline numbers from a real 500-document OTCS corpus.

item.title: Keeping Your OTCS RAG Pipeline Fresh: Incremental Sync with OTModifyDate
item.description: How to detect and re-ingest only changed documents using the modify_date field — with safe-fail timestamp management, deletion reconciliation, and clock-skew buffers.

item.title: Building a Production RAG Pipeline for OpenText Content Server
item.description: End-to-end walkthrough: OTCSTicket auth, document discovery, parent-child chunking, ChromaDB ingestion, and hybrid retrieval with RRF reranking. Written from real production experience.

item.title: How I Debugged 150M Document Download Failures in cs_client.py
item.description: The container flag vs node_type trap, the results vs data key mismatch, and the diagnostic scripts that finally cracked it. Two days, two bugs, one fix each.

item.title: Clean Architecture in Enterprise ECM: What Actually Works
item.description: After 13 years of ECM projects, opinions on abstraction layers, LlamaIndex vs roll-your-own, vendor lock-in, and when to build vs buy.

item.title: xECM for SAP: Architecture Patterns That Actually Work
item.description: After 10+ xECM for SAP engagements — pharma, manufacturing, chemicals — here are the patterns that hold up and the anti-patterns I keep seeing repeated.

item.title: Zero-Downtime ECM Upgrades: Lessons from KSA, Brazil, and Finland
item.description: How to plan and execute OpenText Content Server major version upgrades with minimal outage windows — including the 90% outage reduction at SCG USA.

item.title: Migrating 150M Documents to Kubernetes-Native Content Server
item.description: Architecture, tooling, and hard lessons from the Securex Belgium engagement: moving 150M documents from a legacy ECM system into a cloud-native OTCS deployment, with zero data loss.
